# Meeting 07.12.2023

- Res-NET architecture (easier to justify in publication)

- Verify the model with a minimal toy dataset if we can actually get
 > back results, it should give perfect results in the
 > reconstruction, 10-100 tiles → The model should be able to
 > memorise them and give a perfect reconstruction (irrelevant model
 > but showing that our data is compatible with the model)

- Spherical latent space → could suggest that the model collapsed and
 > went into random reconstruction

- Pre filtering or weighting the empty spaces, so we still have some
 > information out of it, but we can try to get rid of the imbalance

- Newer architectures can allow to use larger patches but would focus
 > on the relevant information within those tiles

- Create different version of the same image and feed them to the
 > architecture

- Direction: Rather using larger data if computationally possible

- [Balance the data]{.mark} by sampling from the distribution (eg.
 > median)

# Notes from the meetings with Esa

# 

# Meeting 07.12.2023

- Res-NET architecture (easier to justify in publication)

- Verify the model with a minimal toy dataset if we can actually get
 > back results, it should give perfect results in the
 > reconstruction, 10-100 tiles → The model should be able to
 > memorise them and give a perfect reconstruction (irrelevant model
 > but showing that our data is compatible with the model)

- Spherical latent space → could suggest that the model collapsed and
 > went into random reconstruction

- Pre filtering or weighting the empty spaces, so we still have some
 > information out of it, but we can try to get rid of the imbalance

- Newer architectures can allow to use larger patches but would focus
 > on the relevant information within those tiles

- Create different version of the same image and feed them to the
 > architecture

- Direction: Rather using larger data if computationally possible

- [Balance the data]{.mark} by sampling from the distribution (eg.
 > median)

```{=html}
<!-- -->
```
- Autoencoders: conceptually makes sense to have a validation set
 > (measuring the loss against it, so we can see that the model is

ution (eg.
 > median)

```{=html}
<!-- -->
```
- Autoencoders: conceptually makes sense to have a validation set
 > (measuring the loss against it, so we can see that the model is
 > rather generalising than memorising) → in practice it seem to
 > doesn\'t matter much, the models usually generalise quite well →
 > [cluster the latent space, show on the images and consult
 > biological experts about the results]{.mark}

- Make the split that we are never re-shuffling the data to keep
 > things clear

- In NKI: leave 20 patients for validation, patients from different
 > groups

> (Maybe 10% per group for validation)

- Quantitative measure for latent space: What would be the loss range
 > we would expect? When the model start to reach that, just eyeball
 > the UMAP of the latent space. We should see clear clusters at this
 > point and bring in experts.

- The channels should be kept together for our research question

- Try vanilla encoder if it reconstruct

- If we want to sample then we need the variational autoencoders

- If it works with vanilla, we could go with that

- VQ-VAE did not work very well for Esa

- Histoencoder is basically contrastive learning (best on cross
 > covariance transformer on Histoencoder)

- We could adapt Histoencoder, the biggest problem would be the number
 > of channels → in histopathology there are a lot of images with

ution (eg.
 > median)

```
- Autoencoders: conceptually makes sense to have a validation set

 > rather generalising than memorising) → in practice it seem to
 > doesn\'t matter much, the models usually generalise quite well →
 > [cluster the latent space, show on the images and consult

- In NKI: leave 20 patients for validation, patients from different

> (Maybe 10% per group for validation)

- Quantitative measure for latent space: What would be the loss range
 > we would expect? When the model start to reach that, just eyeball
 > the UMAP of the latent space. We should see clear clusters at this
 > point and bring in experts.

- The channels should be kept together for our research question

- Try vanilla encoder if it reconstruct

- If we want to sample then we need the variational autoencoders

- If it works with vanilla, we could go with that

- VQ-VAE did not work very well for Esa

- Histoencoder is basically contrastive learning (best on cross
 > covariance transformer on Histoencoder)

- We could adapt Histoencoder, the biggest problem would be the number
 > of channels → in histopathology there are a lot of images with

> covariance transformer on Histoencoder)

- We could adapt Histoencoder, the biggest problem would be the number
 > of channels → in histopathology there are a lot of images with
 > always the same channels, for us we have many channels and they
 > are not the same for every experiment

- Think about the shared channels (typically about 15 markers are very
 > common)

- [Pick random 3 markers, train the model, put it aside, then repeat
 > -- for Histoencoder]{.mark}

# Meeting 27.02.2024

\- Esa suggested using the multi-channel of the whole image

\- The reconstruction was not very sharp. What is the latest dimension?
Is it possible to improve the latent size to 15?

\- Is the title enough to be classified?

\- Are there batch effects?

\- Take all the features of the tiles. Design pooling strategies.

\- Esa suggests randomly sampling the same amount of tiles per image.

\- Anniina suggests predicting the therapy sequence as a downstream
task.

\- Esa suggests concatenating the features from each VAE, as a matrix.
Every row for each tile.

\- Essa suggests plotting ROC, and AUC curves for the classification.

\- For the classification, to make the classification balanced, Esa
suggested to pick randomly the same number of patches from both classes.

\- Esa asked about Resnet

\- Esa proposed to run a lot of triplet models on Mahti, using VEP.

> covariance transformer on Histoencoder)

- We could adapt Histoencoder, the biggest problem would be the number
 > of channels → in histopathology there are a lot of images with
 > always the same channels, for us we have many channels and they
 > are not the same for every experiment

- Think about the shared channels (typically about 15 markers are very
 > common)

- [Pick random 3 markers, train the model, put it aside, then repeat
 > -- for Histoencoder]{.mark}

# Meeting 27.02.2024

\- Esa suggested using the multi-channel of the whole image

\- The reconstruction was not very sharp. What is the latest dimension?
Is it possible to improve the latent size to 15?

\- Is the title enough to be classified?

\- Are there batch effects?

\- Take all the features of the tiles. Design pooling strategies.

\- Esa suggests randomly sampling the same amount of tiles per image.

\- Anniina suggests predicting the therapy sequence as a downstream
task.

\- Esa suggests concatenating the features from each VAE, as a matrix.
Every row for each tile.

\- Essa suggests plotting ROC, and AUC curves for the classification.

\- For the classification, to make the classification balanced, Esa
suggested to pick randomly the same number of patches from both classes.

\- Esa asked about Resnet

\- Esa proposed to run a lot of triplet models on Mahti, using VEP.

nced, Esa
suggested to pick randomly the same number of patches from both classes.

\- Esa asked about Resnet

\- Esa proposed to run a lot of triplet models on Mahti, using VEP.

\- Esa commented that it would also be a good idea to explore the
classification of cores. He commented that samples are not so much, and
also generate the training and validation set.

\- Esa commented that we can also cluster the latent space to discover
new features.

\- Esa suggests running a model for each combination of markers (5,500
models)

nced, Esa
suggested to pick randomly the same number of patches from both classes.

\- Esa asked about Resnet

\- Esa proposed to run a lot of triplet models on Mahti, using VEP.

\- Esa commented that it would also be a good idea to explore the
classification of cores. He commented that samples are not so much, and
also generate the training and validation set.

\- Esa commented that we can also cluster the latent space to discover
